name: resources
context: Define MCP resources — data sources that provide contextual information to AI applications via URIs
info: This sub-skill covers MCP resources — the application-controlled primitive. Resources are data sources exposed by MCP servers that provide contextual information to AI applications. Each resource is identified by a URI (file://, https://, custom schemes) and can contain text or binary data. Resources support listing (resources/list), reading (resources/read), URI templates for parameterized access, subscriptions for change notifications, and annotations for metadata. Python: use @mcp.resource(uri) decorator. TypeScript: use server.resource(). Resources complement tools by providing the data context that informs tool usage.

sections.title: Defining resources
sections.body: Python: `@mcp.resource(uri)` decorator on a function returning string (text) or bytes (binary). URI can be a template with `{param}` placeholders. TypeScript: `server.resource(name, uri, handler)` with ResourceTemplate for parameterized URIs. Resources return `contents` arrays with uri, mimeType, and text or blob.

sections.title: Protocol messages
sections.body: `resources/list` returns available resources with uri, name, description, mimeType. `resources/read` returns resource contents. `resources/templates/list` returns URI templates for parameterized resources. `notifications/resources/list_changed` signals list updates. `notifications/resources/updated` signals individual resource changes.

sections.title: Subscriptions
sections.body: If the server declares `subscribe: true`, clients can subscribe to individual resources via `resources/subscribe`. When a resource changes, the server sends `notifications/resources/updated` with the URI. Clients then re-read the resource. Unsubscribe with `resources/unsubscribe`.

sections.title: URI schemes
sections.body: Standard schemes: `file://` for filesystem resources, `https://` for web resources (client-fetchable), `git://` for version control. Custom schemes (e.g., `schema://`, `config://`, `db://`) are encouraged for application-specific resources. All URIs must conform to RFC 3986.

links.context: Tools perform actions; resources provide context

related.note: Tools act on data; resources provide data

scripts.name: Add Resource to Python Server
scripts.description: Add a resource definition to an existing FastMCP server

depends.reason: Resources are defined within an MCP server

author: dojo-community
